caption:
Stop using JSON.parse(JSON.stringify()) to deep clone objects.

There's a native way now, and it handles things JSON can't.

📌 SAVE this for your next project.

Which method were you using? Comment below 👇

#javascript #webdev #coding #programming #frontend #tips #dev #react #nodejs #typescript #codingtips

slides.title: B wins. Every time.

cards.label: Option A — JSON trick
cards.title: Breaks on Date, Map, Set, RegExp
cards.text: JSON.stringify can't handle circular references, undefined values, Dates (converts to string), Maps, Sets, or RegExp. It silently corrupts your data.

cards.label: Option B — structuredClone
cards.title: Handles everything natively
cards.text: Built into every browser and Node 17+. Copies Date, Map, Set, ArrayBuffer, RegExp, Error objects, and even circular references. Zero dependencies.

slides.takeaway: If you're still using the JSON trick in 2026, stop today.

slides.title: What structuredClone handles
slides.intro: Everything the JSON trick silently breaks:
slides.explanation: All types preserved. No serialization. No data loss.

slides.title: Deep clone cheat sheet

items.text: JSON trick → breaks on Date, Map, Set, RegExp
Spread {...obj} → shallow only, nested refs shared
structuredClone works in all browsers + Node 17+
